title: olearia stellulata var. rugosa
intro: Olearia stellulata var. rugosa is a variant of the Olearia stellulata plant, a type of flowering shrub native to Australia. It is commonly known as the 'starry daisy bush' due to its small, white daisy-like flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are believed to provide relief from skin irritations, wounds, and minor infections. Olearia stellulata var. rugosa can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or salves, and is sometimes used in modern herbal medicine to promote wound healing and soothe skin conditions such as eczema and acne.
